name: Crab Stuffed Prawns with Tomato Butter
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Green bell peppers; finely minced
2 tbsp Red bell peppers; finely minced
1 lb Lump crabmeat; picked over for shells and cartilage
16 Prawns; peeled except for tails and butterflied
3/4 cup Plum tomatoes; seeded and chopped
1 Stick unsalted butter; cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees F. In a sauté pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, sauté the onions, celery, peppers, shallots, and 4 teaspoons of garlic for 2-3 minutes. Add the essence, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon pepper, and crabmeat. Sauté for an additional minute. Remove from heat and let cool. Stir the cooled crab mixture with the egg, cheese, mustard, and bread crumbs. Form the stuffing into 16 balls and season each prawn with remaining essence. Stuff each prawn cavity with a ball of stuffing. Place prawns on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake for about 10 minutes.
Tomato butter: In a small non-reactive saucepot, mash the tomatoes over high heat for 1 minute. Add garlic, shrimp stock, salt, cayenne pepper, and black pepper. Bring to a boil and whisk constantly for 1 minute. Whisk in butter cubes until fully incorporated.
